old_claim.note: Early Copyright & Printing Privileges is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No printing-privilege predecessor remains because the scoped English invention grants begin in 1449, before the 1469 Venetian printing monopoly represented by that node.

source_shape.source_locator: Research-guide introduction dates surviving English and Welsh invention patents from 1449 and separately discusses early grants without specifications.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: English invention privileges are documented before the later Venetian printing privilege used by the removed prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later foreign print monopoly cannot be a historical predecessor of an already documented English invention grant.

ontology_before: English invention privileges depended on a later Venetian copyright and printing privilege.
ontology_after: The English grant tradition is independently anchored to 1449 without importing a later print-rights lineage.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: early_patent_systems no longer directly depends on early_copyright_printing_privileges.
would_reject_if: The invention-privilege node were redated and rescoped to a later system directly derived from printing monopolies.
short_reason: The proposed predecessor postdated the English grant anchor.
